title: Thyrsanthema rotundifolia
taxonomy: Thyrsanthema rotundifolia is classified within the family Xanthorrhoeaceae, and more specifically within the subfamily Hemerocallidoideae. This classification places it among the asphodels, a group of flowering plants. The genus Thyrsanthema is part of the order Asparagales. The family Xanthorrhoeaceae is a large and diverse group of flowering plants, including many species of asphodels and other related plants. Thyrsanthema rotundifolia is native to Western Australia and is often found growing in dry, sandy soils.
uses: Thyrsanthema rotundifolia, also known as the Australian spear grass, is a plant native to Australia. Its medicinal uses include treating various health issues.
